This README is intentionally exhaustive: it documents the current codebase, highlights every implemented feature, consolidates research, and provides a multi-phase plan to reach world-class status.

    56→
    57→## Overview and Mission
    58→- Live site: travisjneuman.com running as a single-page hero-style portfolio.
    59→- Mission: showcase real-world IT expertise (networking, system administration, cloud, software development, automation) with uncompromising accessibility, performance, and credibility.
    60→- Philosophy: privacy-first, accessibility-first, performance-obsessed, secure-by-default, progressively enhanced, intentionally simple foundation that can scale.
    61→- Vision: evolve from a static business card into a full IT services, thought leadership, and community platform with case studies, booking, education, and interactive experiences.
    62→
    63→## Audience and Purpose
    64→- Hiring managers and recruiters seeking proof of hands-on capability, reliability, and breadth across infra and software.
    65→- Clients seeking consulting, systems work, networking design, cloud migrations, automation, and training.
    66→- IT peers looking for tutorials, patterns, tooling notes, and practical writeups grounded in real-world scenarios.
    67→- Community members interested in labs, writeups, and incident retrospectives.
    68→- Future self: a documented, repeatable platform to add content, features, and experiments without losing quality.
    69→
    70→## Current Snapshot (v1)
    71→- Version: 1.0 (MVP) | Status: Active development | Last updated: December 2025.
    72→- Architecture: Single index.html, no build step, vanilla HTML5/CSS3/JavaScript.
    73→- Deployment: GitHub Pages with Cloudflare CDN, custom domain, HTTPS enforced.
    74→- Analytics: Cloudflare Web Analytics (cookie-less) + Google Analytics 4 (IP anonymization, secure cookies).
    75→- SEO: Canonical URL, comprehensive meta tags, Open Graph and Twitter cards, Schema.org Person JSON-LD, robots.txt, sitemap.xml.
    76→- Accessibility: WCAG 2.1 AA targeted; semantic landmarks, skip link, keyboard-friendly nav, ARIA labels, focus visibility; user zoom allowed.
    77→- Assets: Full favicon suite (SVG, ICO, PNG sizes, Apple touch, manifest), branded animated mountain background, overlay textures.
    78→- External dependencies: Google Fonts (Source Sans Pro), local Font Awesome 5 webfonts, Cloudflare beacon script, GA4 script.
    79→
    80→## Key Differentiators
    81→- Hybrid IT capability: networking + systems + cloud + development in one voice, with room to display both infrastructure and code depth.
    82→- Enterprise readiness: emphasize security hygiene (HTTPS, noopener, privacy-first analytics) and future compliance (CSP, Referrer-Policy, data minimization).
    83→- Accessibility-first posture: WCAG AA target from inception, including semantics, focus management, zoom support, skip links, and motion considerations.
    84→- Performance-first posture: minimal JS (~3 lines), optimized assets, hardware-accelerated CSS animations, CDN-backed delivery.
    85→- Research-backed roadmap: built from award-winning portfolios (Bruno Simon, Jesse Zhou, Jordan Cruz-Correa, Steven Mengin, Jan Baszczok) and curated resources (Awwwards, Webflow, Colorlib, Hostinger, Designmodo, Codementor, SiteGround Academy, Fiero Code).
    86→- Documentation-driven: tasks and audits stored in /tasks/, architecture in /docs/, and this README as a living build plan.
    87→
    88→## Current Live Features
    89→### Design and UX
    90→- Responsive layout for desktop/tablet/mobile; centered hero stack with controlled vertical rhythm.
    91→- Animated mountain landscape background with gradient overlay; hardware-accelerated CSS keyframes (60s loop).
    92→- Source Sans Pro typography (weights 300 and 900); clean, minimalist aesthetic.
    93→- Smooth fade-in and slide-up animations on load; noscript fallback ensures content visibility without JS.
    94→- High-contrast color pairing (blue gradient + white text) for readability.
    95→
    96→### Content and Calls-to-Action
    97→- Hero: name, title, and succinct tagline anchored on expertise and collaboration.
    98→- Social nav: LinkedIn, GitHub, email; external links hardened with rel="noopener noreferrer" and descriptive aria-labels.
    99→- Placeholder space for future CTAs (consultation booking, resume download, portfolio PDF, newsletter signup).
   100→

[![Performance](https://img.shields.io/badge/Performance-Optimized-yellow?style=for-the-badge)](https://travisjneuman.com)

> A modern, privacy-first professional portfolio evolving into a comprehensive IT services, learning, and community platform. This README is intentionally exhaustive: it documents the current codebase, highlights every implemented feature, consolidates research, and provides a multi-phase plan to reach world-class status.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **🚧 V2 MIGRATION IN PROGRESS 🚧**
> A complete rewrite of this portfolio is currently underway in the [`/new`](./new) directory.
> - **Root Directory:** Contains the legacy v1 site (HTML/CSS/JS).
> - **`/new` Directory:** Contains the new v2 site (Greenfield build).
> - **Do NOT modify root files** unless critical. All new feature development should happen in `/new`.

## Overview and Mission
- Live site: travisjneuman.com running as a single-page hero-style portfolio.
- Mission: showcase real-world IT expertise (networking, system administration, cloud, software development, automation) with uncompromising accessibility, performance, and credibility.
- Philosophy: privacy-first, accessibility-first, performance-obsessed, secure-by-default, progressively enhanced, intentionally simple foundation that can scale.
- Vision: evolve from a static business card into a full IT services, thought leadership, and community platform with case studies, booking, education, and interactive experiences.

## Audience and Purpose
- Hiring managers and recruiters seeking proof of hands-on capability, reliability, and breadth across infra and software.
- Clients seeking consulting, systems work, networking design, cloud migrations, automation, and training.
- IT peers looking for tutorials, patterns, tooling notes, and practical writeups grounded in real-world scenarios.
- Community members interested in labs, writeups, and incident retrospectives.
- Future self: a documented, repeatable platform to add content, features, and experiments without losing quality.

## Current Snapshot (v1)
- Version: 1.0 (MVP) | Status: Active development | Last updated: December 2025.
- Architecture: Single index.html, no build step, vanilla HTML5/CSS3/JavaScript.
- Deployment: GitHub Pages with Cloudflare CDN, custom domain, HTTPS enforced.
- Analytics: Cloudflare Web Analytics (cookie-less) + Google Analytics 4 (IP anonymization, secure cookies).
- SEO: Canonical URL, comprehensive meta tags, Open Graph and Twitter cards, Schema.org Person JSON-LD, robots.txt, sitemap.xml.
- Accessibility: WCAG 2.1 AA targeted; semantic landmarks, skip link, keyboard-friendly nav, ARIA labels, focus visibility; user zoom allowed.
- Assets: Full favicon suite (SVG, ICO, PNG sizes, Apple touch, manifest), branded animated mountain background, overlay textures.
- External dependencies: Google Fonts (Source Sans Pro), local Font Awesome 5 webfonts, Cloudflare beacon script, GA4 script.

## Key Differentiators
- Hybrid IT capability: networking + systems + cloud + development in one voice, with room to display both infrastructure and code depth.
- Enterprise readiness: emphasize security hygiene (HTTPS, noopener, privacy-first analytics) and future compliance (CSP, Referrer-Policy, data minimization).
- Accessibility-first posture: WCAG AA target from inception, including semantics, focus management, zoom support, skip links, and motion considerations.
- Performance-first posture: minimal JS (~3 lines), optimized assets, hardware-accelerated CSS animations, CDN-backed delivery.
- Research-backed roadmap: built from award-winning portfolios (Bruno Simon, Jesse Zhou, Jordan Cruz-Correa, Steven Mengin, Jan Baszczok) and curated resources (Awwwards, Webflow, Colorlib, Hostinger, Designmodo, Codementor, SiteGround Academy, Fiero Code).
- Documentation-driven: tasks and audits stored in /tasks/, architecture in /docs/, and this README as a living build plan.

## Current Live Features
### Design and UX
- Responsive layout for desktop/tablet/mobile; centered hero stack with controlled vertical rhythm.
- Animated mountain landscape background with gradient overlay; hardware-accelerated CSS keyframes (60s loop).
- Source Sans Pro typography (weights 300 and 900); clean, minimalist aesthetic.
- Smooth fade-in and slide-up animations on load; noscript fallback ensures content visibility without JS.
- High-contrast color pairing (blue gradient + white text) for readability.

### Content and Calls-to-Action
- Hero: name, title, and succinct tagline anchored on expertise and collaboration.
- Social nav: LinkedIn, GitHub, email; external links hardened with rel="noopener noreferrer" and descriptive aria-labels.
- Placeholder space for future CTAs (consultation booking, resume download, portfolio PDF, newsletter signup).

     2→
     3→**Project:** Travis J. Neuman Portfolio
     4→**Date:** 2025-11-23
     5→**Version:** 1.0
     6→**Status:** Active Development
     7→
     8→---

    22→## Project Overview
    23→
    24→### Purpose
    25→Professional IT portfolio website serving as a digital business card and professional showcase for Travis J. Neuman.
    26→
    27→### Tech Stack
    28→- **HTML5** - Semantic markup

   106→
   107→---
   108→
   109→## Architecture Decisions
   110→
   111→### 1. Single-Page Architecture
   112→
   113→**Decision:** Single `index.html` file with no additional pages
   114→**Rationale:**
   115→- Simple, fast-loading design
   116→- Minimal maintenance overhead
   117→- Perfect for portfolio/business card use case
   118→- No routing complexity
   119→
   120→**Trade-offs:**
   121→- ✅ Pros: Instant load, no navigation lag, simple deployment
   122→- ⚠️ Cons: Limited content depth (addressed in Phase 2+ expansion)
   123→
   124→**Future:** Multi-page structure planned for Phase 3-4 (projects, about, contact)
   125→
   126→---
   127→
   128→### 2. No Build Process / No Framework
   129→
   130→**Decision:** Vanilla HTML/CSS/JS with no build tools or frameworks
   131→**Rationale:**
   132→- Maximum simplicity and portability

# Project Structure & Architecture Documentation

**Project:** Travis J. Neuman Portfolio
**Date:** 2025-11-23
**Version:** 1.0
**Status:** Active Development

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Project Overview](#project-overview)
2. [Directory Structure](#directory-structure)
3. [Architecture Decisions](#architecture-decisions)
4. [File Descriptions](#file-descriptions)
5. [Build & Deployment](#build--deployment)
6. [Development Workflow](#development-workflow)
7. [Future Scalability](#future-scalability)

---

## Project Overview

### Purpose
Professional IT portfolio website serving as a digital business card and professional showcase for Travis J. Neuman.

### Tech Stack
- **HTML5** - Semantic markup
- **CSS3** - Styling with animations
- **Vanilla JavaScript** - Minimal, no framework dependencies
- **Font Awesome 5** - Icon library
- **Google Fonts** - Typography (Source Sans Pro)

### Hosting
- **Platform:** GitHub Pages / Custom hosting
- **Domain:** travisjneuman.com
- **CDN:** Cloudflare (implied from analytics)
- **SSL:** Enabled (HTTPS)

### Version
**Current:** v1.0 - Minimal MVP
**Next:** v2.0 - Content expansion (Phase 2-7 roadmap)

---

## Directory Structure

```
tjn.portfolio/
│
├── .git/                          # Git repository (version control)
├── .gitignore                     # Git ignore patterns
│
├── index.html                     # ⭐ Main entry point (8.2KB)
├── robots.txt                     # Search engine directives
├── sitemap.xml                    # Search engine sitemap
│
├── CLAUDE.md                      # Development guidelines & AI instructions
├── README.md                      # Project documentation (17.7KB)
│
├── assets/                        # Static assets directory
│   ├── css/                       # Stylesheets
│   │   ├── main.css               # ⭐ Primary stylesheet (23KB)
│   │   ├── noscript.css           # No-JavaScript fallback styles
│   │   ├── fontawesome-all.min.css # Font Awesome icons (56KB)
│   │   └── images/                # CSS-referenced images
│   │       ├── ColoredMountainsLoop.jpg  # Background animation (1.1MB)
│   │       ├── overlay-pattern.png       # Texture overlay (14KB)
│   │       └── overlay.svg               # Gradient overlay (787B)
│   │
│   ├── favicons/                  # Favicon suite (7.4MB total)
│   │   ├── favicon.svg            # ⭐ Primary favicon (7.4MB high-quality)
│   │   ├── favicon.ico            # Legacy browser support (15KB)
│   │   ├── favicon-96x96.png      # Modern browsers (2.7KB)
│   │   ├── apple-touch-icon.png   # iOS home screen (5.8KB)
│   │   ├── web-app-manifest-192x192.png  # PWA small (7KB)
│   │   ├── web-app-manifest-512x512.png  # PWA large (32KB)
│   │   └── site.webmanifest       # PWA manifest (459B)
│   │
│   ├── sass/                      # SASS source files (optional)
│   │   ├── main.scss              # SASS source for main.css (8.7KB)
│   │   └── noscript.scss          # SASS source for noscript.css (474B)
│   │
│   ├── social/                    # Social media assets
│   │   └── README.md              # Social media graphic specifications
│   │
│   └── webfonts/                  # Font Awesome webfonts
│       ├── fa-brands-400.woff2    # Brand icons (75KB)
│       ├── fa-regular-400.woff2   # Regular icons (14KB)
│       └── fa-solid-900.woff2     # Solid icons (75KB)
│
├── docs/                          # Documentation (this file)
│   └── PROJECT_STRUCTURE.md       # This document
│
└── tasks/                         # Task management & planning
    ├── .archive/                  # Completed tasks archive
    │   ├── codebase-cleanup-audit.md
    │   └── cleanup-followup-audit.md
    │
    ├── portfolio-comprehensive-readme-and-roadmap.md  # Master roadmap
    ├── analytics-seo-social-enhancement.md            # Analytics/SEO plan
    ├── accessibility-audit-phase1.md                  # A11y audit
    ├── google-search-console-setup.md                 # GSC guide
    └── social-media-validation-guide.md               # Social testing guide
```

---

## Architecture Decisions

### 1. Single-Page Architecture

**Decision:** Single `index.html` file with no additional pages
**Rationale:**
- Simple, fast-loading design
- Minimal maintenance overhead
- Perfect for portfolio/business card use case
- No routing complexity

**Trade-offs:**
- ✅ Pros: Instant load, no navigation lag, simple deployment
- ⚠️ Cons: Limited content depth (addressed in Phase 2+ expansion)

**Future:** Multi-page structure planned for Phase 3-4 (projects, about, contact)

---

### 2. No Build Process / No Framework

**Decision:** Vanilla HTML/CSS/JS with no build tools or frameworks
**Rationale:**
- Maximum simplicity and portability
- No npm dependencies to maintain
- No build step required
- Direct file editing and deployment

**Trade-offs:**
- ✅ Pros: Zero dependencies, instant changes, no compile time
- ⚠️ Cons: No TypeScript, no module bundling, manual optimization

**Future:** May add Vite build process in Phase 5-7 for advanced features

---

### 3. Static File Hosting

**Decision:** Static files deployed directly to hosting (GitHub Pages/CDN)
**Rationale:**
- No server-side processing needed
- Maximum performance and caching

1→# React + TypeScript + Vite
     2→
     3→This template provides a minimal setup to get React working in Vite with HMR and some ESLint rules.
     4→
     5→Currently, two official plugins are available:
     6→
     7→- [@vitejs/plugin-react](https://github.com/vitejs/vite-plugin-react/blob/main/packages/plugin-react) uses [Babel](https://babeljs.io/) (or [oxc](https://oxc.rs) when used in [rolldown-vite](https://vite.dev/guide/rolldown)) for Fast Refresh
     8→- [@vitejs/plugin-react-swc](https://github.com/vitejs/vite-plugin-react/blob/main/packages/plugin-react-swc) uses [SWC](https://swc.rs/) for Fast Refresh

    10→## React Compiler

    14→## Expanding the ESLint configuration
    15→
    16→If you are developing a production application, we recommend updating the configuration to enable type-aware lint rules:
    17→
    18→```js
    19→export default defineConfig([
    20→  globalIgnores(['dist']),
    21→  {
    22→    files: ['**/*.{ts,tsx}'],
    23→    extends: [
    24→      // Other configs...

    26→      // Remove tseslint.configs.recommended and replace with this
    27→      tseslint.configs.recommendedTypeChecked,
    28→      // Alternatively, use this for stricter rules
    29→      tseslint.configs.strictTypeChecked,

1→# ✈️ V2 Implementation Plan: The "Hybrid Minimalist" Build
     2→
     3→**Status:** In Progress
     4→**Objective:** Build a world-class, "Senior Engineer" portfolio in `/new` using React 19, Vite, Tailwind, and Framer Motion.
     5→
     6→---
     7→
     8→## 🏗️ Phase 1: Foundation & Scaffolding (Current)
     9→- [x] **Initialize Project:** Scaffold React+Vite in `/new`.
    10→- [x] **Install Dependencies:** Tailwind, Framer Motion, Lenis, cmdk, Lucide.
    11→- [x] **Configure Tailwind:** Set up `tailwind.config.js` with colors, fonts, and animations.
    12→- [ ] **Configure Fonts:** Install/Setup `Geist Sans` and `Geist Mono`.
    13→- [x] **Directory Structure:** Create `components/ui`, `components/layout`, `hooks`, `lib`.
    14→- [x] **Global Styles:** Reset CSS and set up CSS variables for dark mode.
    15→
    16→## 🧱 Phase 2: The Shell (Layout & Navigation)
    17→- [x] **Smooth Scroll:** Implement `Lenis` wrapper component.
    18→- [x] **Navigation:** Build the "Dynamic Island" or Sticky Nav with blur effect.
    19→- [x] **Command Palette:** Implement `cmdk` modal (Cmd+K) for global navigation.
    20→- [ ] **Footer:** Create the "Let's Work Together" reveal footer.

## COMPREHENSIVE CODEBASE EXPLORATION REPORT

### 1. PROJECT PURPOSE AND TYPE

This is **Travis J. Neuman's Professional IT Portfolio** website - a modern, privacy-first digital presence designed to showcase an IT professional's expertise across multiple domains (networking, system administration, cloud, software development, automation). The project is evolving from a minimal business-card MVP (v1) into a world-class, comprehensive professional platform (v2).

**Live Site:** travisjneuman.com
**Repository:** GitHub (travisjneuman/tjn.portfolio)
**Current Status:** Active Development - V2 Migration In Progress
**Project Phase:** Early-stage greenfield build with mature v1 foundation

---

### 2. TECHNOLOGY STACK

#### **V1 (Current Production - Legacy)**
- **Frontend:** HTML5, CSS3, Vanilla JavaScript
- **Build:** No build process - direct file serving
- **Styling:** CSS3 with SASS source files (optional preprocessing)
- **Framework:** None (100% vanilla)
- **Icons:** Font Awesome 5 (webfonts)
- **Typography:** Google Fonts (Inter/Source Sans Pro)
- **External Scripts:**
  - Cloudflare Web Analytics (cookie-less)
  - Google Analytics 4 (IP anonymization)

#### **V2 (In Development - Greenfield)**
- **Build Tool:** Vite 7.2.4
- **Frontend Framework:** React 19.2.0
- **Language:** TypeScript 5.9 (strict mode)
- **Styling:** Tailwind CSS 4.1 + PostCSS
- **CSS Architecture:** Semantic tokens with dark mode support

- **UI Components:** Lucide React (icons)

- **Package Manager:** npm (Node.js)

- **Semantic HTML:** Proper landmarks (header, nav, main, footer), ARIA labels, skip links
